title: Running an image produces `Exec format error`
body: **Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** Podman prints this error when running the following image (happens with certain other images too) ```sh podman run -ti alpine/k8s:1.22.9 ``` Returns ``` {"msg":"exec container process `/bin/sh`: Exec format error","level":"error","time":"2022-06-21T17:18:15.000049555Z"} ``` The same things works well with docker. My assumption is that it related to me being on a Mac with an M1 chip and the image is not available for `linux/amd64`. **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Get a Mac with an M1 chip 2. Run the command `podman run -ti alpine/k8s:1.22.9` 3. See the error message (after the image is pulled) **Describe the results you received:** Got an error **Describe the results you expected:** For it to run **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** I think it's related to the image not being available for `linux/amd64` but the same works for docker **Output of `podman version`:** ``` podman version 4.1.1 ``` **Output of `podman info --debug`:** ``` host: arch: arm64 buildahVersion: 1.26.1 cgroupControllers: - cpu - io - memory - pids cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: conmon-2.1.0-2.fc36.aarch64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.1.0, commit: ' cpuUtilization: idlePercent: 99.88 systemPercent: 0.06 userPercent: 0.06 cpus: 1 distribution: distribution: fedora variant: coreos version: "36" eventLogger: journald hostname: localhost.localdomain idMappings: gidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 100000 size: 1000000 uidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1872712432 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 100000 size: 1000000 kernel: 5.17.5-300.fc36.aarch64 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: journald memFree: 159838208 memTotal: 2053337088 networkBackend: netavark ociRuntime: name: crun package: crun-1.4.4-1.fc36.aarch64 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.4.4 commit: 6521fcc5806f20f6187eb933f9f45130c86da230 spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL os: linux remoteSocket: exists: true path: /run/user/1872712432/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: true seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json selinuxEnabled: true serviceIsRemote: true slirp4netns: executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns package: slirp4netns-1.2.0-0.2.beta.0.fc36.aarch64 version: |- slirp4netns version 1.2.0-beta.0 commit: 477db14a24ff1a3de3a705e51ca2c4c1fe3dda64 libslirp: 4.6.1 SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3 libseccomp: 2.5.3 swapFree: 0 swapTotal: 0 uptime: 14h 27m 38.42s (Approximately 0.58 days) plugins: log: - k8s-file - none - passthrough - journald network: - bridge - macvlan volume: - local registries: search: - docker.io store: configFile: /var/home/core/.config/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 29 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 29 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: {} graphRoot: /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage graphRootAllocated: 106825756672 graphRootUsed: 4768964608 graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: xfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp imageStore: number: 19 runRoot: /run/user/1872712432/containers volumePath: /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 4.1.0 Built: 1651853798 BuiltTime: Fri May 6 18:16:38 2022 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.18 Os: linux OsArch: linux/arm64 Version: 4.1.0 ``` **Package info (output of `brew info podman`):** ``` podman: stable 4.1.1 (bottled), HEAD Tool for managing OCI containers and pods https://podman.io/ /opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/4.1.1 (174 files, 46.4MB) * Poured from bottle on 2022-06-21 at 16:27:54 From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/podman.rb License: Apache-2.0 ==> Dependencies Build: go ✘, go-md2man ✘ Required: qemu ✔ ==> Options --HEAD Install HEAD version ==> Caveats Bash completion has been installed to: /opt/homebrew/etc/bash_completion.d ==> Analytics install: 19,919 (30 days), 60,377 (90 days), 161,015 (365 days) install-on-request: 19,749 (30 days), 60,164 (90 days), 160,781 (365 days) build-error: 4 (30 days) ``` **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)** Yes **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** On a Mac with an M1 chip

comments.body: If the image is not available for amd64 you have to install qemu-user-static to support x86_64 emulation, see https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/12899

Ok, thanks for pointing that out. Nice to see there is a discussion for adding built-in support for this.

Depending how long this takes, maybe something could be done about the error message. `Exec Format Error` is quite misleading and something like `This image is not supported for your architecture. Please see ...` would help understanding what the issue is (and might probably have prevented me from opening this issue)
I think the next podman version (v4.2) should display a warning about the incorrect image arch.
